candidate_sources_rejected: traumatic-wound summaries without exact guideline sections
rejection_reasons: Patient pages are not clinician guidance.
Unopened summaries were not used for item-level claims.
Commercial dressing guidance was not authoritative.
population_applicability: Partial: exact for surgical and postoperative wounds; traumatic wounds, scar review, suture removal, and every wound subtype are not fully covered.
setting_applicability: Exact for postoperative, community, and specialist wound care; partial for telehealth-only or traumatic-wound assessment.
UAE_applicability: No exact UAE wound-review guideline was identified; NICE content requires UAE clinician review and local pathways.
recency_verification: The current official NICE NG125 recommendations, last updated 2020 and reviewed in 2023, were reopened on 2026-07-12.
superseded_check: NICE NG125 remains current and replaces CG74.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped wound-care information, infection-warning, return, and contact documentation only when discussed by the clinician.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports clinician-documented postoperative wound-care information, infection-warning discussion, and who to contact if concern develops.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped postoperative wound identity, dressing, structured care, and clinician-documented management fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Defines the postoperative surgical-wound population and supports wound dressing, cleansing, wound-healing, and structured postoperative wound-care documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports mapped pain, redness, swelling, discharge, wound-edge, fever, microbiology, and suspected-infection documentation fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports review of suspected infection, cellulitis, microbiology context, structured wound care, and local heat, redness, pain, swelling, discharge, separation, abscess, fever, or systemic signs.

unresolved_source_gaps: Traumatic mechanism, tetanus, suture removal, scar assessment, necrosis, immunocompromise, wound measurement, blood testing, and fixed one-to-three-day review timing remain unsupported.
The wound diagnosis and any infection assessment remain clinician-stated.
Qualified clinician review has not been completed.
